Accepting new students
Coached by Ganesh R · FIDE 2100+ · 24+ Years
FIDE Rated

International FIDE Rated Player & Coach

Build a confident,
thinking chess player

From strong foundations to tournament success.

From their very first move to competitive tournaments — your child gets personal attention, a structured learning path, and a coach who has trained students from 14 countries to win at state, national and international level.

Every student learns at a different pace, so the coaching is adjusted based on the child's level, understanding, and response during class. This allows for closer guidance, timely correction of mistakes, and more meaningful progress over time.

The training follows a proper learning path instead of random topics, so students build their understanding step by step. This gives parents more clarity, helps children avoid confusion, and creates a stronger long-term foundation in chess.

The coaching approach is built on practical improvement, with students showing visible growth in confidence, understanding, ratings, and tournament performance. It is not only about teaching concepts, but about helping students apply them effectively in real games.

Students from different countries and backgrounds have trained under this coaching approach, which brings wider experience and adaptability in teaching. This helps create a professional learning environment while still giving each student the attention they need.

Train with purpose.Compete with confidence.

Coaching Offered

Programs for Serious Improvement

Designed for beginners, growing tournament players, and families looking for a structured path in chess.

1:1 Personal Coaching

Customized training based on the student’s level, strengths, and improvement areas, with focused guidance in every class.

Group Classes

Interactive sessions that combine concepts, guided practice, and structured learning in a motivating group environment.

Tournament Preparation

Specialized preparation for competitive players, including openings, strategy, game review, and practical tournament thinking.

About Ganesh

FIDE Rated Coach.
Structured Mentor.

Ganesh combines deep chess experience with a patient, methodical teaching approach that helps students understand the game clearly and grow with confidence.

With more than two decades in chess, Ganesh has worked with students across multiple countries and different levels of competition. His coaching style focuses on foundations first, then builds toward stronger tournament thinking, better decision-making, and long-term improvement.

Parents value the personal attention, calm guidance, and structured approach he brings to each student. The goal is not only to teach moves, but to build stronger thinkers who can handle real games with confidence.

What We Cover

Training Structure

A comprehensive curriculum designed for measurable progress at every level.

01
🎯

Game Analysis

Deep review of your own tournament games — finding patterns, mistakes, and missed opportunities specific to your play style.

02
⚡

Tactical & Calculation Training

Systematic puzzles and calculation exercises to sharpen your combinative vision and speed.

03
♜

Strategy & Planning

Positional concepts, pawn structures, piece coordination — building a deep strategic foundation.

04
🏁

Endgame Mastery

Foundational to advanced endgame techniques — turning slight edges into decisive wins.

05
🎲

Activity-Based Learning

Engaging, interactive methods that make complex ideas stick and keep students motivated.

06
💬

Practical Play & Feedback

Real games followed by immediate, actionable coaching feedback to accelerate improvement.

Simple Process

How It Works

Getting started is straightforward. Three steps from first contact to weekly training.

1
📝

Fill the Application

Share your child's current level, age, and goals through the short student application form. This takes less than 3 minutes.

2
🎯

Free Trial Session

A 30-minute trial session to understand your child's thinking process, strengths, and the right training path — before any commitment.

3
♟

Personalised Weekly Training

Structured weekly sessions tailored to your child's level and goals. Progress is tracked and parents are kept informed throughout.

FAQ

Questions Parents Commonly Ask

These answers are designed to make the coaching journey clearer before you begin.

What is the ideal age to start learning chess?

Many children can begin as early as 4 or 5 if they are ready to listen, engage, and enjoy the learning process. The right start depends more on interest and focus than age alone.

How do you assess a student’s level?

The trial session includes a short interaction to understand the student’s basics, thinking process, and current level so the right coaching path can be suggested.

Are classes suitable for complete beginners?

Yes. The coaching is designed to build strong foundations first, so even beginners can learn in a clear and structured way without feeling overwhelmed.

How soon can a child start playing tournaments?

That depends on the student’s readiness, but after a solid period of working on basics, tactics, and confidence, tournament participation can be introduced step by step.

Will there be homework and game review?

Yes. Practice tasks, homework ideas, and game analysis can be used to support improvement outside class and make the training more effective.

How do we begin?

Start with a 30-minute trial session. Fill out the enquiry form, share the student details, and the next step will be guided clearly.

Get In Touch

Apply for Personalized Training

Start with the student application first. It helps Ganesh understand the student’s current level, thinking process, and learning goals before suggesting the right training path.

Recommended First Step

Apply for Personalized Training

This short application makes the enrollment process clearer for parents and more personalized for each student from the very beginning.

Before suggesting a training plan, I first understand the student's current level, thinking process, strengths, and areas that need improvement. This helps ensure that the coaching is not random, but guided in the right direction from the beginning.

Every student learns differently, so the training is shaped around their goals, whether it is building strong basics, improving tournament performance, or gaining confidence in practical play. This gives parents a clearer sense of purpose and helps the student progress step by step.

Each student receives close guidance based on their pace, understanding, and learning style. This allows me to support their improvement more carefully, correct mistakes early, and build confidence in a more meaningful way.
